title: B2 Level Test — The Ultimate Professional English Test
titleTr: B2 Seviye Testi — Nihai Profesyonel Ingilizce Testi
description: This is it — the final B2 test! Demonstrate your mastery of all B2 grammar, vocabulary, idioms, collocations, and professional communication. Live through Eyüp's complete professional week: board meeting, client call, team workshop, conference, and Friday reflection.
descriptionTr: Iste bu — B2 final testi! Tum B2 dilbilgisi, kelime bilgisi, deyimler, kolokasyonlar ve profesyonel iletisim becerilerini goster. Eyup'un tam bir profesyonel haftasini yasa: yonetim kurulu toplantisi, musteri aramasi, ekip calistayi, konferans ve Cuma degerlendirmesi.

vocabulary.example: In retrospective, the decision to enter the Asian market was the right one.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Geriye donuk degerlendirildiginde, Asya pazarina giris karari dogru olaniydi.
vocabulary.extra: The team held a retrospective to analyze what went well and what didn't.
vocabulary.extraTr: Ekip neyin iyi gittigini ve neyin gitmedigini analiz etmek icin geriye donuk degerlendirme yapti.

vocabulary.example: In hindsight, we should have secured funding six months earlier.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Simdi geriye baktigimizda, alti ay once finansman saglamamiz gerekiyordu.
vocabulary.extra: Hindsight is always 20/20 — it's easy to see mistakes after they happen.
vocabulary.extraTr: Geriye bakis her zaman nettir — hatalar olduktan sonra gormek kolaydir.

vocabulary.example: His perseverance through difficult negotiations earned the respect of the entire board.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Zor muzakereler boyunca gosterdigi azim, tum yonetim kurulunun saygisini kazandi.
vocabulary.extra: Success is not about talent alone — it's about perseverance and consistency.
vocabulary.extraTr: Basari sadece yetenekle ilgili degildir — azim ve tutarlilikla ilgilidir.

vocabulary.example: Learning collocations like 'drive growth' makes your English sound more natural.
vocabulary.exampleTr: 'Drive growth' gibi kolokasyonlari ogrenmek Ingilizce'nizi daha dogal yapar.
vocabulary.extra: Native speakers use collocations instinctively — B2 learners should study them actively.
vocabulary.extraTr: Anadili konusucular kolokasyonlari icgudusel kullanir — B2 ogrencileri onlari aktif olarak calismalidir.

vocabulary.example: The idiom 'back to the drawing board' means starting over from the beginning.
vocabulary.exampleTr: 'Back to the drawing board' deyimi bastan baslamak anlamina gelir.
vocabulary.extra: Using idioms correctly in meetings shows a high level of English fluency.
vocabulary.extraTr: Toplantilarda deyimleri dogru kullanmak yuksek duzey Ingilizce akiciligi gosterir.

vocabulary.example: We set new benchmarks for customer satisfaction this year.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Bu yil musteri memnuniyeti icin yeni referans degerler belirledik.
vocabulary.extra: If we hadn't used industry benchmarks, we wouldn't have known where we stood.
vocabulary.extraTr: Sektor referans degerlerini kullanmasaydik, nerede oldugunu bilemezdik.

vocabulary.example: All key stakeholders were consulted before the final decision was made.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Nihai karar verilmeden once tum kilit paydaslara danildi.
vocabulary.extra: Stakeholder management is a critical skill for any project manager.
vocabulary.extraTr: Paydas yonetimi herhangi bir proje yoneticisi icin kritik bir beceridir.

vocabulary.example: You should adjust your register depending on whether you're speaking to a client or a colleague.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Bir musteri mi yoksa bir meslektasla mi konustugunuza bagli olarak dil kaydinizi ayarlamalisiniz.
vocabulary.extra: The register used in a board meeting differs significantly from casual team chat.
vocabulary.extraTr: Yonetim kurulu toplantisinda kullanilan uslup, gundelik ekip sohbetinden onemli olcude farklidir.

vocabulary.example: In business English, we hedge our statements: 'It might be worth considering...' instead of 'You should do this.'
vocabulary.exampleTr: Is Ingilizcesinde ifadelerimizi yumusatiriz: 'Bunu yapmalisiniz' yerine 'Dusunmeye deger olabilir...'
vocabulary.extra: Hedging language helps you sound diplomatic and avoid confrontation.
vocabulary.extraTr: Yumusatma dili diplomatik durmaniza ve catismadan kacinmaniza yardimci olur.

vocabulary.example: There's an important nuance between 'assertive' and 'aggressive' in professional communication.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Profesyonel iletisimde 'assertive' ve 'aggressive' arasinda onemli bir ince ayrim vardir.
vocabulary.extra: Understanding cultural nuances is essential when doing business internationally.
vocabulary.extraTr: Uluslararasi is yaparken kulturel nüanslari anlamak gereklidir.

vocabulary.example: The CEO's rhetoric during the keynote inspired confidence among investors.
vocabulary.exampleTr: CEO'nun konusma sirasindaki retorigi yatirimcilar arasinda guven yaratti.
vocabulary.extra: Effective rhetoric combines logic, emotion, and credibility.
vocabulary.extraTr: Etkili retorik mantik, duygu ve guvenilirligi birlestirir.

vocabulary.example: We need to take a more strategic approach to entering new markets.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Yeni pazarlara girmeye daha stratejik bir yaklasim benimsememiz gerekiyor.
vocabulary.extra: Strategic thinking is what separates managers from leaders.
vocabulary.extraTr: Stratejik dusunme, yoneticileri liderlerden ayiran seydir.

vocabulary.example: Her analytical skills helped the team identify the root cause of the problem.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Analitik becerileri ekibin sorunun temel nedenini belirlemesine yardimci oldu.
vocabulary.extra: An analytical mindset is crucial when measuring performance and interpreting data.
vocabulary.extraTr: Performansi olcerken ve verileri yorumlarken analitik bir zihin yapisi kritiktir.

vocabulary.example: The report provides a comprehensive overview of our financial performance.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Rapor, finansal performansimizin kapsamli bir genel gorunumunu sunuyor.
vocabulary.extra: A comprehensive analysis requires looking at data from multiple perspectives.
vocabulary.extraTr: Kapsamli bir analiz, verilere birden fazla perspektiften bakmay gerektirir.

vocabulary.turkish: acik ve etkili ifade etmek / duzgun konusan
vocabulary.example: She articulated her vision so clearly that everyone understood the strategy.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Vizyonunu o kadar acik ifade etti ki herkes stratejiyi anladi.
vocabulary.extra: Being articulate in English meetings requires both vocabulary and confidence.
vocabulary.extraTr: Ingilizce toplantilarda acik ifade etmek hem kelime bilgisi hem de ozguven gerektirir.

vocabulary.example: His eloquent speech at the conference left a lasting impression on the audience.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Konferanstaki etkileyici konusmasi dinleyicilerde kalici bir iz birakti.
vocabulary.extra: An eloquent presenter can turn a boring topic into an engaging story.
vocabulary.extraTr: Etkileyici bir sunucu, sikici bir konuyu ilgi cekici bir hikayeye donusturebilir.

vocabulary.example: She's proficient in three languages, which makes her invaluable in international negotiations.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Uc dilde yetkin, bu da onu uluslararasi muzakerelerde paha bicilmez kiliyor.
vocabulary.extra: To become proficient, you need consistent practice, not just theoretical knowledge.
vocabulary.extraTr: Yetkin olmak icin sadece teorik bilgi degil, tutarli pratik gerekir.

vocabulary.example: A competent manager knows when to delegate and when to take action personally.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Yetkin bir yonetici ne zaman gorev devredecegini ve ne zaman bizzat harekete gececegini bilir.
vocabulary.extra: There's a nuance between 'competent' (meets standards) and 'proficient' (exceeds standards).
vocabulary.extraTr: 'Competent' (standartlari karsilar) ve 'proficient' (standartlari aser) arasinda ince bir ayrim vardir.

vocabulary.turkish: cok yonlu / esnek / her ise yarayan
vocabulary.example: We need versatile team members who can adapt to different roles and challenges.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Farkli roller ve zorluklara uyum saglayabilen cok yonlu ekip uyelerine ihtiyacimiz var.
vocabulary.extra: A versatile professional is valued in today's rapidly changing business environment.
vocabulary.extraTr: Cok yonlu bir profesyonel, gunumuzun hizla degisen is ortaminda deger gorur.

vocabulary.example: She's incredibly resourceful — she found a solution when everyone else had given up.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Inanilmaz becerikli — herkes pes etmisken bir cozum buldu.
vocabulary.extra: Resourceful employees navigate challenges without waiting for instructions.
vocabulary.extraTr: Becerikli calisanlar talimat beklemeden zorluklari asarlar.

vocabulary.example: A tactful response during a heated debate can prevent escalation.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Hararetli bir tartisma sirasinda ince dusunceli bir yanit tIrmanmayi onleyebilir.
vocabulary.extra: Being tactful doesn't mean avoiding the truth — it means delivering it with respect.
vocabulary.extraTr: Ince dusunceli olmak gercekten kacinmak degil — onu saygiyla iletmek demektir.

vocabulary.example: His diplomatic approach to the conflict helped both parties reach a compromise.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Catismaya diplomatik yaklasimi her iki tarafin da bir uzlasIya varmasina yardimci oldu.
vocabulary.extra: In international business, being diplomatic is more important than being right.
vocabulary.extraTr: Uluslararasi iste diplomatik olmak hakli olmaktan daha onemlidir.

vocabulary.example: You need to be assertive in negotiations without being aggressive.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Muzakerelerde agresif olmadan kararli olmaniz gerekir.
vocabulary.extra: Assertive communication means stating your position clearly while respecting others.
vocabulary.extraTr: Kararli iletisim, baskalarina saygi gosterirken pozisyonunuzu acikca belirtmek demektir.

vocabulary.example: A decisive leader doesn't hesitate when the data supports a clear course of action.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Kararli bir lider, veriler acik bir eylem yolunu desteklediginde tereddut etmez.
vocabulary.extra: Being decisive saved us from missing the investment opportunity.
vocabulary.extraTr: Kararli olmak bizi yatirim firsatini kacirmaktan kurtardi.

vocabulary.example: Every team member should be accountable for their deliverables.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Her ekip uyesi teslimatlarindan sorumlu olmalidir.
vocabulary.extra: In a culture of accountability, people own their mistakes and learn from them.
vocabulary.extraTr: Hesap verebilirlik kulturunde insanlar hatalarini sahiplenir ve onlardan ogrenir.

vocabulary.example: Transparent communication builds trust between management and employees.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Seffaf iletisim yonetim ve calisanlar arasinda guven insa eder.
vocabulary.extra: Investors prefer companies that are transparent about their financial performance.
vocabulary.extraTr: Yatirimcilar finansal performanslari konusunda seffaf olan sirketleri tercih eder.

vocabulary.example: Business integrity means doing the right thing even when no one is watching.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Is ahlaki, kimse izlemese bile dogru olanI yapmak demektir.
vocabulary.extra: A company with integrity attracts both customers and talent.
vocabulary.extraTr: Durustluge sahip bir sirket hem musterileri hem de yetenekleri ceker.

vocabulary.example: You establish credibility by consistently delivering results and meeting deadlines.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Surekli sonuc sunarak ve son tarihlere uyarak guvenilirlik olusturursunuz.
vocabulary.extra: Once you lose credibility, it takes years to rebuild it.
vocabulary.extraTr: Guvenilirligi kaybettiginizde, yeniden insa etmek yillar alir.

vocabulary.example: Our reputation for excellence is our most valuable asset.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Mukemmellik konusundaki itibarimiz en degerli varligimizdir.
vocabulary.extra: It takes 20 years to build a reputation and five minutes to ruin it.
vocabulary.extraTr: Bir itibar insa etmek 20 yil, yikmak bes dakika surer.

vocabulary.example: Our pursuit of excellence drives everything we do, from product design to customer service.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Mukemmellik arayisimiz, urun tasarimindan musteri hizmetine kadar yaptigimiz her seyi yonlendirir.
vocabulary.extra: Excellence is not a destination — it's a continuous journey of improvement.
vocabulary.extraTr: Mukemmellik bir varil noktasi degildir — surekli bir iyilestirme yolculugudur.

grammar.title: B2 Complete Grammar Review
grammar.explanation: This is your comprehensive B2 grammar review covering everything from Past Perfect and Third Conditional to Mixed Conditionals, Gerunds/Infinitives, Advanced Passive, Collocations, Idioms, Wish/If only, Register, Essay structure, Debate language, and News language. Master these and you've earned your B2 badge!
grammar.explanationTr: Bu, Past Perfect ve Ucuncu Kosul'dan Karisik Kosullara, Gerund/Infinitive'lere, Ileri Edilgen Cati'ya, Kolokasyonlara, Deyimlere, Wish/If only'ye, Dil Kaydina, Deneme yapisina, Tartisma diline ve Haber diline kadar her seyi kapsayan kapsamli B2 dilbilgisi tekrarindir. Bunlarda ustala ve B2 rozetini kazanmis ol!
table: Past Perfect: had + past participle — completed before another past event
By the time the investors arrived, Eyüp had already prepared the presentation.
The team had exceeded targets before the quarter ended.
Use Past Perfect when one past action happened BEFORE another past action.
Third Conditional: If + had + PP, would have + PP — unreal past
If we had secured funding earlier, we would have expanded into Asia last year.
If she hadn't been so decisive, the company would have missed the opportunity.
Talks about unreal past — things that DIDN'T happen. 'Had we invested...' is formal inversion.
Mixed Conditionals: Past condition → present result OR present condition → past result
If we had invested in AI two years ago, we would be market leaders now. (past→present)
If he were more diplomatic, he wouldn't have offended the client yesterday. (present→past)
Mix tenses to connect past causes with present effects, or present traits with past outcomes.
Gerunds & Infinitives: verb + -ing vs. verb + to + base form
We considered expanding into Latin America. (consider + -ing)
We decided to expand into Latin America. (decide + to + inf)
Some verbs take -ing (enjoy, consider, avoid), some take to (decide, plan, expect), some take both with meaning change (stop, remember, try).
Advanced Passive: have something done, get + PP, It is said that...
We had the contract reviewed by an external lawyer. (causative)
It is widely believed that the merger will be completed by Q3.
Causative: 'have/get something done' = someone else does it for you. Impersonal passive for news/reports.
Wish / If only: wish + past simple (present), wish + past perfect (past regret)
I wish I were more articulate in English meetings. (present wish)
If only we had mitigated the risks earlier! (past regret)
Wish + past simple = I want something different NOW. Wish + past perfect = I regret something in the PAST.
Register, Idioms & Collocations: formal vs informal, natural word combinations
Formal: 'I would like to draw your attention to...' Informal: 'Check this out.'
Idiom: 'back to the drawing board.' Collocation: 'drive growth.'
B2 speakers switch registers, use idioms naturally, and know which words collocate.

tips.en: Third Conditional formal inversion: 'Had we known about the risks, we would have mitigated them.' Drop 'if' and invert — sounds very professional.
tips.tr: Ucuncu Kosul resmi ters cevirme: 'Riskleri bilseydik, onlari azaltirdik.' 'If' kaldir ve ters cevir — cok profesyonel duyulur.

tips.en: Mixed Conditionals connect past and present: 'If I had studied harder at university, I would be a CEO now.' Past cause → present result.
tips.tr: Karisik Kosullar gecmis ve simdiki zamani baglar: 'Universitede daha cok calissaydim, simdi CEO olurdum.' Gecmis neden → simdiki sonuc.

tips.en: Gerund trap: 'I stopped to smoke' (paused in order to smoke) vs 'I stopped smoking' (quit the habit). Meaning changes completely!
tips.tr: Gerund tuzagi: 'I stopped to smoke' (sigara icmek icin durdum) vs 'I stopped smoking' (sigarayi bIraktim). Anlam tamamen degisiyor!

tips.en: Causative passive: 'We had the report audited' = we didn't audit it ourselves — someone else did it FOR us. Very common in business.
tips.tr: Ettirgen edilgen: 'Raporu denetlettirdik' = biz kendimiz denetlemedik — baskasi bizim icin yapti. Iste cok yaygin.

tips.en: Register switch: Email to CEO → 'I would appreciate your guidance on this matter.' Slack to colleague → 'Hey, quick question about this.' Know when to switch!
tips.tr: Uslup degisimi: CEO'ya e-posta → 'Bu konuda yonlendirmenizi takdir ederim.' Meslektasa Slack → 'Selam, bununla ilgili kisa bir soru.' Ne zaman degistirecegini bil!

tips.en: Chain everything in one sentence to sound B2: 'Had we leveraged technology earlier, we would be driving growth now, and stakeholders wouldn't have raised concerns.' Third + Mixed + Collocation!
tips.tr: B2 duymak icin her seyi bir cumleye zincirle: 'Teknolojiyi daha once kullansaydik, simdi buyume sagliyor olurduk ve paydaslar endise dile getirmezdi.' Ucuncu + Karisik + Kolokasyon!

reading.text:
MY JOURNEY FROM B1 TO B2 — A Comprehensive Reflection

When I completed B1, I felt proud but also aware of how much more there was to learn. B1 had given me the tools to survive in English — B2 has given me the tools to thrive. Looking back, the journey from B1 to B2 was not just about learning new grammar and vocabulary. It was about developing a completely new way of thinking and communicating.

At B1, I could participate in meetings, write professional emails, and handle basic negotiations. But there were moments when I wished I were more articulate, when I struggled to find the right nuance, and when my register was sometimes too formal or too casual. If I had known at the start of B2 how much my communication would improve, I wouldn't have been so anxious.

The grammar journey was intense. Past Perfect helped me tell stories about business events in proper sequence. Third Conditional taught me to analyze past decisions: 'Had we entered the market earlier, we would have captured more share.' Mixed Conditionals connected my past actions to present realities. Gerunds and infinitives — which I had always found confusing — finally clicked when I practiced them in real business contexts.

But vocabulary was where the real transformation happened. I went from knowing individual words to understanding collocations — 'drive growth,' 'secure funding,' 'mitigate risks.' I learned idioms that made my English sound natural: 'back to the drawing board,' 'the ball is in your court,' 'cut corners.' I discovered the power of register — knowing when to say 'I would like to propose' versus 'How about we try this?'

The professional scenarios were the most valuable part. I presented to investors, navigated board meetings, facilitated workshops, debated strategies, and delivered conference presentations — all in English. Had someone told me two years ago that I would be doing these things, I would have laughed. In hindsight, perseverance was the key. There were weeks when I felt no progress, when every new grammar rule seemed to contradict the previous one. But I kept going.

What I've learned about myself through this journey is perhaps more important than the English itself. I've become more analytical in my thinking, more diplomatic in my communication, and more decisive in my actions. I've learned that being competent is good, but being proficient requires going beyond the comfort zone. I've learned that integrity and transparency in communication build credibility faster than any eloquent speech.

As I look toward C1, I know the challenges ahead will be even greater. Advanced academic writing, sophisticated debate techniques, and near-native fluency await. But I've established a strong foundation. If I were to give advice to someone starting B2, I would say this: embrace the complexity, be resourceful when you're stuck, and remember that excellence is not a destination — it's a continuous journey. B2 complete. C1, I'm ready for you.
reading.textTr:
B1'DEN B2'YE YOLCULUGUM — Kapsamli Bir Degerlendirme

B1'i tamamladigimda gurur duydum ama ayni zamanda ne kadar cok sey ogrenecegimin de farkinaydim. B1 bana Ingilizce'de hayatta kalma araclarini vermisti — B2 bana gelisme araclarini verdi. Geriye baktigimda, B1'den B2'ye yolculuk sadece yeni dilbilgisi ve kelime bilgisi ogrenmekle ilgili degildi. Tamamen yeni bir dusunme ve iletisim sekli gelistirmekle ilgiliydi.

B1'de toplantilara katilabiliyordum, profesyonel e-postalar yazabiliyordum ve temel muzakereleri yonetebiliyordum. Ama daha acik ifade etmeyi diledikam, dogru nüansi bulmakta zorlandigim ve uslubumun bazen cok resmi bazen cok yakin oldugu anlar vardi. B2'nin basinda iletisimimin ne kadar iyilesecegini bilseydim, bu kadar endiselenmezdim.

Dilbilgisi yolculugu yogundu. Past Perfect is olaylarini dogru sirada anlatmama yardimci oldu. Ucuncu Kosul gecmis kararlari analiz etmemi ogretti: 'Pazara daha once girseydik, daha fazla pay elde ederdik.' Karisik Kosullar gecmis eylemlerimi simdiki gerceklikle bagladi. Her zaman kafa karistirici buldugum gerund ve infinitive'ler, onlari gercek is baglamlarinda pratik ettigimde nihayet oturdu.

Ama asil donusum kelime bilgisinde gerceklesti. Tek tek kelime bilmekten kolokasyonlari anlamaya gectim — 'drive growth,' 'secure funding,' 'mitigate risks.' Ingilizce'mi dogal yapan deyimler ogrendim: 'back to the drawing board,' 'the ball is in your court,' 'cut corners.' Uslubun gucunu kesfettim — ne zaman 'I would like to propose' ne zaman 'How about we try this?' diyecegimi bilmek.

Profesyonel senaryolar en degerli kisimdi. Yatirimcilara sunum yaptim, yonetim kurulu toplantilarinda bulundum, calistaular yonettim, stratejileri tartistim ve konferans sunumlari yaptim — hepsi Ingilizce. Iki yil once biri bunlari yapacagimi soylseydi, gulerdim. Geriye baktigimda, azim anahtardi. Ilerleme hissetmedigim, her yeni dilbilgisi kuralinin bir oncekiyle celisir gorunmek haftalar oldu. Ama devam ettim.

Bu yolculuk boyunca kendim hakkinda ogrendigim sey belki de Ingilizce'nin kendisinden daha onemlidir. Dusunmemde daha analitik, iletisimimde daha diplomatik ve eylemlerimde daha kararli oldum. Yetkin olmanin iyi oldugunu ama usta olmanin konfor alaninin otesine gec gerektirdigini ogrendim. Iletisimde durustluk ve seffafligin guvenilirligi her turlu etkileyici konusmadan daha hizli insa ettigini ogrendim.

C1'e dogru bakarken, ondeki zorluklarin daha da buyuk olacagini biliyorum. Ileri akademik yazim, sofistike tartisma teknikleri ve anadile yakin akicilik bekliyor. Ama guclu bir temel olusturdm. B2'ye baslayan birine tavsiye verecek olsam sunu soylerdim: karmasikligi benimse, takIldiginda becerikli ol ve muKemmEelligin bir varil noktasi degil surekli bir yolculuk oldugunu hatirla. B2 tamam. C1, sana hazirim.

questions.question: According to the text, what was the difference between B1 and B2 for Eyüp?
questions.questionTr: Metne gore, B1 ve B2 arasindaki fark Eyup icin neydi?
options: B1 gave survival tools, B2 gave thriving tools

questions.question: What grammar structure is used in: 'Had we entered the market earlier, we would have captured more share'?
questions.questionTr: 'Pazara daha once girseydik, daha fazla pay elde ederdik' cumlesinde hangi yapi kullanilmis?

questions.question: What does Eyüp say was the key to his success from B1 to B2?
questions.questionTr: Eyup, B1'den B2'ye basarisinin anahtarinin ne oldugunu soyluyor?

questions.question: Which three types of word knowledge does Eyüp mention as transformative?
questions.questionTr: Eyup donusturucu olarak hangi uc kelime bilgisi turunden bahsediyor?

questions.question: What advice does Eyüp give to someone starting B2?
questions.questionTr: Eyup B2'ye baslayan birine ne tavsiye veriyor?
options: Embrace complexity, be resourceful, pursue excellence

fillBlanks.sentence: By the time the investors arrived, Eyüp ___ already prepared the presentation.
If we ___ secured funding earlier, we would have expanded last year.
If I had studied harder at university, I ___ be a CEO now.
We considered ___ into three new markets simultaneously.
The contract was ___ reviewed by an external legal team.
I wish I ___ more articulate during yesterday's meeting.
If only we ___ mitigated the risks earlier!
fillBlanks.hint: If only + past perfect — past regret

fillBlanks.sentence: ___ we invested in AI two years ago, we would be market leaders now.
She stopped ___ emails at midnight for better work-life balance.
It is widely ___ that the merger will be completed by Q3.
fillBlanks.hint: Impersonal passive — It is + PP + that

fillBlanks.sentence: His ___ during difficult negotiations earned the board's respect.
You should adjust your ___ depending on your audience.
In ___, we should have entered the market six months earlier.
Our pursuit of ___ drives everything we do.

multipleChoice.question: "___ we known about the risks, we would have mitigated them." Dogru basi?
"If I had accepted the job offer, I ___ living in London now." Mixed Conditional?
"We decided ___ operations into Latin America." Dogru form?
"We had the report ___ by an external auditor." Causative passive?
"I wish I ___ more decisive during the negotiation." Present wish?
"If only we ___ leveraged technology earlier!" Past regret?
"She avoided ___ the difficult topic during the meeting." Dogru form?
"It is said ___ the CEO will resign next month." Impersonal passive?
"I stopped ___ notes." (I paused in order to take notes) Dogru olan?
"If he ___ more diplomatic, he wouldn't have offended the client." Mixed Conditional (present→past)?
"She ___ her vision so clearly that everyone understood." Dogru fiil?
"Back to the drawing board" ne demek?
"A ___ leader doesn't hesitate when data supports a clear action." Dogru sifat?

sentenceOrder.correct: Had we secured funding earlier we would have expanded
If I had studied harder I would be a CEO now
We considered expanding into three new markets
The contract was had reviewed by an external lawyer
I wish I were more articulate in meetings
If only we had mitigated the risks earlier
It is widely believed that the merger will succeed
Perseverance was the key to his success
A decisive leader doesn't hesitate when data supports action
Excellence is not a destination it's a continuous journey

translation.english: Had we known about the risks, we would have mitigated them.
acceptAlso: If we had known about the risks, we would have mitigated them

translation.turkish: Universitede daha cok calissaydim, simdi CEO olurdum.
translation.english: If I had studied harder at university, I would be a CEO now.
acceptAlso: Had I studied harder at university, I would be a CEO now

translation.english: We considered expanding into Latin America.
acceptAlso: We considered expanding our operations into Latin America

translation.english: We had the contract reviewed by an external lawyer.
acceptAlso: The contract was reviewed by an external lawyer

translation.turkish: Keske toplantilarda daha acik ifade edebilsem.
translation.english: I wish I were more articulate in meetings.
acceptAlso: I wish I could be more articulate in meetings

translation.english: If only we had mitigated the risks earlier!
acceptAlso: I wish we had mitigated the risks earlier

translation.english: Perseverance was the key to his success.
acceptAlso: Perseverance was key to his success

translation.turkish: Kararli bir lider veriler eylemi desteklediginde tereddut etmez.
translation.english: A decisive leader doesn't hesitate when data supports action.
acceptAlso: A decisive leader does not hesitate when data supports a clear course of action

translation.turkish: Mukemmellik bir varil noktasi degil, surekli bir yolculuktur.
translation.english: Excellence is not a destination — it's a continuous journey.
acceptAlso: Excellence is not a destination, it's a continuous journey of improvement

translation.turkish: Birlesmenin basarili olacagina yaygin olarak inaniliyor.
translation.english: It is widely believed that the merger will succeed.
acceptAlso: It is widely believed that the merger will be successful

dialog.title: Eyüp's Complete Professional Week
dialog.context: Eyup'un B2 seviyesindeki tam bir is haftasi: Pazartesi yonetim kurulu toplantisi (Mr. Henderson, baskan), Sali musteri aramasi (Ms. Nakamura, Japonya'dan), Carsamba ekip calistayi (Selin, Kerem, Ayse), Persembe konferans sunumu (moderator Sarah), Cuma degerlendirme. Birden fazla konusmaci, farkli usluplar.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Good morning, everyone. Welcome to our Q1 board meeting. We have a comprehensive agenda today. Eyüp, could you start with the financial overview?
lines.textTr: Gunaydin millet. 1. ceyrek yonetim kurulu toplantimiza hosgeldiniz. Bugun kapsamli bir gundemimiz var. Eyup, finansal genel gorunumle baslar misin?

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Thank you, Mr. Henderson. I'd like to draw your attention to three key metrics. Revenue grew by 32% year-over-year. We had exceeded our annual target before Q4 even started. Furthermore, by reducing overheads and leveraging technology, we increased margins from 15% to 22%.
lines.textTr: Tesekkurler Bay Henderson. Dikkatinizi uc temel metrike cekmek istiyorum. Gelir yillik bazda %32 buyudu. 4. ceyrek baslamadan once yillik hedefimizi asmistik. Ayrica, genel giderleri azaltarak ve teknolojiyi kullanarak marjlari %15'ten %22'ye cikardik.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Impressive numbers. Had we not made the strategic decision to enter the European market last year, we wouldn't be in this strong position now. What about the risks we discussed last quarter?
lines.textTr: Etkileyici rakamlar. Gecen yil Avrupa pazarina girme stratejik kararini almasaydik, simdi bu guclu pozisyonda olmazdik. Gecen ceyrek tartistigimiz riskler ne oldu?

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: We've mitigated the supply chain risks by diversifying our suppliers. We also had our compliance framework audited by an external firm, and they confirmed we meet all regulatory benchmarks. I wish we had started this process earlier, but we're now fully compliant.
lines.textTr: Tedarikcilarimi cesitlendirerek tedarik zinciri risklerini azalttik. Ayrica uyumluluk cercevemizi harici bir firmaya denetlettirdik ve tum duzenleyici referans degerleri karsiladigimizi teyit ettiler. Keske bu sureci daha once baslatsaydik ama artik tamamen uyumluyuz.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Excellent. Now, regarding the Series B funding — Ms. Chen confirmed her investment yesterday. That means we've secured the full $15 million. Well done, Eyüp.
lines.textTr: Mukemmel. Simdi, Seri B finansmanla ilgili — Bayan Chen dun yatirimini teyit etti. Bu, 15 milyon dolarin tamamini saglamisiz demek. Aferin, Eyup.

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Thank you. We plan to allocate resources in phases — Southeast Asia first, then the Middle East. If we hadn't built such strong credibility with Ms. Chen during the presentation, she might not have committed so quickly.
lines.textTr: Tesekkurler. Kaynaklari asamali olarak tahsis etmeyi planliyoruz — once Guneydogu Asya, sonra Orta Dogu. Sunum sirasinda Bayan Chen ile bu kadar guclu guvenilirlik kurmasaydik, bu kadar cabuk taahhut etmeyebilirdi.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Good afternoon, Eyüp. Thank you for accommodating the time difference. I've reviewed the proposal your team sent, and I have some concerns about the delivery timeline.
lines.textTr: Iyi gunler, Eyup. Saat farkini goz onunde bulundurdugunuz icin tesekkurler. Ekibinizin gonderdigi teklifi inceledim ve teslimat takvimi hakkinda bazi enriselerim var.

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Of course, Ms. Nakamura. I appreciate your transparency. Could you elaborate on which aspects concern you? We want to make sure we meet your expectations.
lines.textTr: Tabii ki, Bayan Nakamura. Seffafliginizi takdir ediyorum. Hangi yonlerin sizi endise ettirdigini detaylandirir misiniz? Beklentilerinizi karsiladigimizdan emin olmak istiyoruz.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: The Phase 2 rollout seems ambitious for a six-month window. If your team were to encounter technical difficulties, would you still be able to deliver on time? I'd like you to hedge that commitment a little.
lines.textTr: Faz 2 yayilimi alti aylik bir pencere icin iddiali gorunuyor. Ekibiniz teknik zorluklarla karsilassa, yine de zamaninda teslim edebilir misiniz? Bu taahhudi biraz yumusatmanizi istiyorum.

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: That's a fair point. Let me be transparent — if we were to face unexpected challenges, we have a contingency plan that adds a two-week buffer. However, based on our benchmarks from the European rollout, we're confident in the timeline. Had we not already navigated similar challenges in Europe, I would share your concern.
lines.textTr: Hakli bir nokta. Seffaf olayim — beklenmedik zorluklarla karsilassak, iki haftalik tampon ekleyen bir acil durum planımız var. Ancak, Avrupa yayilimindaki referans degerlerimize dayanarak, takvime guveniyoruz. Avrupa'da benzer zorluklari daha once asmasaydik, endisenizi paylasirdim.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: I appreciate the nuanced response. That gives me confidence. Let's proceed, and I look forward to the monthly progress reports.
lines.textTr: Nüansli yanitinizi takdir ediyorum. Bu bana guven veriyor. Devam edelim ve aylik ilerleme raporlarini dort gozle bekliyorum.

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Absolutely. We'll make sure every report is comprehensive and delivered on schedule. Thank you for your partnership, Ms. Nakamura.
lines.textTr: Kesinlikle. Her raporun kapsamli ve zamaninda teslim edilmesini saglayacagiz. Ortakliginiz icin tesekkurler, Bayan Nakamura.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Alright team, welcome to our Wednesday innovation workshop. Today's topic: How can we foster innovation while maintaining quality? Eyüp, kick us off.
lines.textTr: Tamam ekip, Carsamba inovasyon calIstaymiza hosgeldiniz. Bugunun konusu: Kaliteyi korurken inovasyonu nasil tesvik edebiliriz? Eyup, sen baslat.

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Thanks, Selin. I've been thinking about this a lot. We need to embrace change, but not at the cost of what already works. I'd suggest we consider allocating 15% of each sprint to experimental features — that way we generate fresh ideas without disrupting core operations.
lines.textTr: Tesekkurler Selin. Bu konuyu cok dusunuyordum. Degisimi benimsememiz gerekiyor ama zaten isleyen seylerin pahasina degil. Her sprintin %15'ini deneysel ozelliklere ayirmayi dusunmemizi oneririm — boylece cekirdek operasyonlari bozmadan taze fikirler ueretiriz.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: I like that. But how do we measure whether the experimental features are worth keeping? We need some kind of benchmark.
lines.textTr: Bunu begendim. Ama deneysel ozelliklerin tutulmaya deger olup olmadigini nasil olceriz? Bir tur referans degere ihtiyacimiz var.

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Great question, Kerem. I'd propose we measure performance using three KPIs: user adoption rate, impact on customer retention, and contribution to revenue. If a feature doesn't meet these benchmarks after 90 days, we go back to the drawing board.
lines.textTr: Harika soru, Kerem. Performansi uc KPI kullanarak olcmemizi oneriyorum: kullanici benimseme orani, musteri elde tutma uzerindeki etki ve gelire katki. Bir ozellik 90 gun sonra bu referans degerleri karsilamazsa, bastan baslariz.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: I'd like to add something. We should also consider getting feedback directly from stakeholders. Sometimes the most resourceful ideas come from the people closest to the customer.
lines.textTr: Bir sey eklemek istiyorum. Dogrudan paydaslardan geri bildirim almayi da dusunmeliyiz. Bazen en becerikli fikirler musteriye en yakin insanlardan gelir.

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Absolutely, Ayşe. In fact, if we had gathered stakeholder feedback earlier on the last product update, we would have avoided three major bugs. Let's make it a standard part of the process. Everyone accountable, everyone heard.
lines.textTr: Kesinlikle, Ayse. Aslinda, son urun guncellemesinde paydas geri bildirimini daha once toplasakydik, uc buyuk hatayi onlerdik. Bunu surecin standart bir parcasi yapalim. Herkes hesap verebilir, herkes dinlenmis olsun.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Welcome to the Global Logistics Technology Summit. Our next speaker is Eyüp from NovaTech Solutions, who will present on 'Scaling with Integrity: How to Drive Growth Without Cutting Corners.' Please welcome Eyüp.
lines.textTr: Kuresel Lojistik Teknoloji Zirvesi'ne hosgeldiniz. Bir sonraki konusmacimiz NovaTech Solutions'tan Eyup, 'Durustlukle Olceklendirme: Koselerden Kismadan Buyume Nasil Saglanir' konusunu sunacak. Lutfen Eyup'u karsilayalim.

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Thank you, Sarah. Good afternoon, everyone. It is widely acknowledged that scaling operations is the holy grail of business growth. But here's the question that keeps me up at night: Can you scale without compromising integrity?
lines.textTr: Tesekkurler Sarah. Iyi gunler herkese. Operasyonlari olceklendirmenin is buyumesinin kutsal kasesi oldugu genis capta kabul gormektedir. Ama beni gece uyutmayan soru su: Durustlukten odun vermeden olceklendirebilir misiniz?

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: At NovaTech, we proved it's possible. We scaled from 3 markets to 12 in just 18 months. Revenue tripled. But — and this is the nuance that matters — we didn't cut corners. We maintained quality by having every process audited quarterly. We established credibility through transparent reporting. And we attracted talent by building a reputation for excellence.
lines.textTr: NovaTech'te bunun mumkun oldugunu kanitladik. Sadece 18 ayda 3 pazardan 12'ye olceklendik. Gelir ucle katlandi. Ama — ve onemli olan nüans bu — koselerden kismadik. Her sureci uc ayda bir denetlettirerek kaliteyi koruduk. Seffaf raporlama araciligiyla guvenilirlik olusturduk. Ve mukemmellik itibari insa ederek yetenek cektik.

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Had we prioritized speed over integrity, we might have grown faster in the short term. But in hindsight, our approach built something sustainable. As the saying goes — it takes 20 years to build a reputation and five minutes to ruin it. My advice? Be strategic, be analytical, be comprehensive in your planning. But above all — be honest. Thank you.
lines.textTr: Hizi durustlugun onune koysaydik, kisa vadede daha hizli buyuyebilirdik. Ama geriye baktigimizda, yaklasimimiz surdurulebilir bir sey insa etti. Soylendigi gibi — bir itibar insa etmek 20 yil, yikmak bes dakika surer. Tavsiyem? Stratejik olun, analitik olun, planlamanizda kapsamli olun. Ama her seyden once — durustolun. Tesekkurler.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Brilliant presentation, Eyüp. We have time for one question from the audience.
lines.textTr: Muhtesem sunum, Eyup. Izleyicilerden bir soru icin zamanIniz var.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Eyüp, you mentioned 'mitigating risks.' Could you be more specific about how you balanced risk-taking with caution during the expansion?
lines.textTr: Eyup, 'riskleri azaltmak'tan bahsettiniz. Genisleme sirasinda risk almayi temkinlilikle nasil dengelediginiz konusunda daha spesifik olabilir misiniz?

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Great question. We used a phased approach — entering one market at a time, measuring performance rigorously, and only proceeding when benchmarks were met. We also had our strategy reviewed by external advisors. Being decisive is important, but being reckless is not the same as being bold. Calculated risks, not blind ones.
lines.textTr: Harika soru. Asamali bir yaklasim kullandik — bir seferde bir pazara girerek, performansi titizlikle olcerek ve yalnizca referans degerleri karsilandiginda ilerleyerek. Ayrica stratejimizi harici danismanlara inceletirdik. Kararli olmak onemlidir ama pervasiz olmak cesur olmakla ayni sey degildir. Hesaplanmis riskler, kor riskler degil.

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: What a week. Monday's board meeting — we secured the full funding. Had we not prepared so thoroughly, the outcome would have been different. Tuesday — Ms. Nakamura's call taught me the value of being tactful and transparent simultaneously. Wednesday's workshop reminded me that the best ideas come from versatile, resourceful teams who feel accountable. Thursday's conference — standing on that stage, delivering a presentation in English about integrity and excellence — if only the Eyüp from two years ago could see me now. In hindsight, every difficult grammar lesson, every awkward conversation, every moment of doubt led to this. B2 is not just a language level. It's a mindset. I'm articulate, I'm assertive, I'm decisive. I've built credibility, reputation, and real competence. The journey continues — C1 awaits. But tonight, I celebrate. I've earned it.
lines.textTr: Ne haftaymis. Pazartesi yonetim kurulu toplantisi — tam finansmani sagladik. Bu kadar kapsamli hazirlananasaydik, sonuc farkli olurdu. Sali — Bayan Nakamura'nin aramasi bana ayni anda ince dusunceli ve seffaf olmanin degerini ogretti. Carsamba calistayi bana en iyi fikirlerin hesap verebilir hisseden cok yonlu, becerikli ekiplerden geldigini hatirlatti. Persembe konferansi — o sahnede durup durustluk ve mukemmellik hakkinda Ingilizce sunum yapmak — iki yil onceki Eyup beni simdi gorebilseydi keske. Geriye baktigimda, her zor dilbilgisi dersi, her garil konusma, her suphe ani buna yol acti. B2 sadece bir dil seviyesi degildir. Bir zihin yapisidir. Acik ifade edebiliyorum, kararlIyim, karar verebiliyorum. Guvenilirlik, itibar ve gercek yetkinlik insa ettim. Yolculuk devam ediyor — C1 bekliyor. Ama bu gece kutluyorum. Hak ettim.

questions.question: How much did revenue grow year-over-year according to Eyüp's board meeting report?
questions.questionTr: Eyup'un yonetim kurulu toplantisi raporuna gore gelir yillik bazda ne kadar buyudu?

questions.question: What grammar structure is used in: 'Had we not made the strategic decision, we wouldn't be in this position now'?
questions.questionTr: 'Stratejik karari almasaydik, simdi bu pozisyonda olmazdik' cumlesinde hangi yapi kullanilmis?

questions.question: What did Ms. Nakamura ask Eyüp to do regarding the Phase 2 commitment?
questions.questionTr: Bayan Nakamura Faz 2 taahhdu konusunda Eyup'tan ne yapmasini istedi?
options: Extend the deadline by six months

questions.question: What idiom did Eyüp use in the workshop when discussing failed features?
questions.questionTr: Eyup calIstayda basarisiz ozellikleri tartisirken hangi deyimi kullanmisti?
options: The ball is in your court

questions.question: In the conference presentation, what did Eyüp say about the relationship between speed and integrity?
questions.questionTr: Konferans sunumunda Eyup hiz ve durustluk arasindaki iliski hakkinda ne soyledi?
options: Prioritizing speed over integrity leads to short-term gains but isn't sustainable
